lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: Have you spoken to any other members of the Gardening Group yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I haven't had a chance yet.

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: Well please hurry, otherwise I won't be able to make enough produce to feed my 13 children soon.

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: Hello, [player name], you look familiar. Didn't I catch you going through my pockets not that long ago?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Me? No, I'm afraid you must have me confused with somebody else!

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: I could have sworn ...

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: Oh well, never mind. I have bigger problems to worry about right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: I can't chat now, I have too many things to worry about.

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: I certainly could do with some help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So tell me about your problems.

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: It all seemed to start about a month ago. I grow roses, you know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um, you seem to be getting side-tracked already.

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: What? Oh sorry.

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: As I was saying about a month ago I noticed that the yield on my prize rose bushes had dropped slightly. I immediately suspected Crown Gall, a disease that can be fatal to a Mister Lincoln.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're not making much sense. Who's Mister Lincoln?

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: Sorry, Mister Lincoln's are the variety of roses that I specialise in. None of the plants showed any other symptoms of disease though. Then I noticed a reduced yield occurring in most of my other crops too.

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: I spoke to other gardeners that I know, and they had all noticed similar problems.

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: Vegetable patches are growing fewer vegetables, bushes are producing fewer berries and everything that is planted seems to be more prone to disease!

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: If you were more interested in turning your hand to farming, you would have noticed it too.

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: You've done some farming, haven't you noticed a decrease in the amount of produce you are getting lately?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do you want me to do?

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: I'd like you to find out what is causing this terrible problem and to try and rectify whatever the problem is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any ideas where I should start?

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: If I were you I would start by speaking to some of the other gardeners.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This doesn't sound like something I can help with. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: I'm not sure I can, but I guess I have to try.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is the banner for?

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: That banner is the emblem of the Group of Advanced Gardeners. We're a loosely affiliated group of gardeners that are united in our concern for declining vegetable production.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, of course you are.

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: Say, you're a brave adventurer, would you be interested in trying to get to the root of our woes? There would be a cash reward if you could help us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course I'll help.

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: I'm so glad to hear that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't look it.

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: Sorry, what was that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, nothing. Don't worry about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So tell me about your problems.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I have better things to do.

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: Oh. Oh dear. Oh well, if there's nothing I can do to make you change your mind...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, nothing. My mind is made up.

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: Well come back and chat to me if you change it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you a member of the Group of Advanced Gardeners?

lines.speaker: [Gardener]
lines.text: Why, yes I am. Why do you ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Martin has asked me to investigate why all the crops are failing.

lines.speaker: [Gardener]
lines.text: No, I'm afraid I don't know anything about that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've spoken to one of your members and they reckon that it's the lack of rain that we've had recently.

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: I'm afraid that can't be the problem. It never rains on the farming patches. That's why we have to water the crops.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, yeah. I hadn't thought of that.

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: Go chat to some other group members. Maybe somebody has a different idea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you a member of the Group of Advanced Gardeners?

lines.speaker: [Gardener #1]
lines.text: Why, yes I am. Why do you ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Martin has asked me to investigate why all the crops are failing.

lines.speaker: [Gardener #1]
lines.text: Ah, Martin sent you, why didn't you say so? I personally think that our problems are all down to the fact that we haven't had enough rain of late. It's plain to see - just look at the levels of the rivers.

lines.speaker: [Gardener #1]
lines.text: We need more rain. I don't suppose you can do anything about that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm afraid not.

lines.speaker: [Gardener #1]
lines.text: Well there you go then. Until you find out how to make it rain you're going to have a hard time solving our problems.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Insects! You need to get rid of the insects! They're eating the crops.

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: Good idea, but I spray my crops with insecticide every week. No, that's not the problem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you a member of the Group of Advanced Gardeners?

lines.speaker: [Gardener #2]
lines.text: Why, yes I am. Why do you ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Martin has asked me to investigate why all the crops are failing.

lines.speaker: [Gardener #2]
lines.text: Insects! That's what you can tell him! Insects! Stop the insects from eating so many of our crops and we'd get maximum yield, I tell you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: One of your members is implying that the gardeners' problems are all caused by adventurers using the plots. I would like to point out that these plots are communal property and that we have as much right as anyone to

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: grow plants here!

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: All right! All right! You're absolutely right! I never said there was a problem with anybody using my plots. Besides, we get part of our income from watching crops for adventurers. I'm afraid that our problem is

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: something totally different.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you a member of the Group of Advanced Gardeners?

lines.speaker: [Gardener #3]
lines.text: Why, yes I am. Why do you ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Martin has asked me to investigate why all the crops are failing.

lines.speaker: [Gardener #3]
lines.text: How can you ask such a thing? It's obvious! It's all you adventurers. Every time I get a crop planted, some adventurer comes along and digs it up and plants crops of their own, as if they owned my farming patch!

lines.speaker: [Gardener #3]
lines.text: It's really annoying! You should speak to your friends about their rude behaviour!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But...but...they're not my friends! I've never even spoken to most of them!

lines.speaker: [Gardener #3]
lines.text: Don't give me that! You're an adventurer, they're all adventurers. I bet you have some sort of secret guild and you gather there and say things like 'let's annoy the gardeners this week', don't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I mean yes. There is a heroes guild, but ...!

lines.speaker: [Gardener #3]
lines.text: Aha, so you admit it then! You should be ashamed of yourselves!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I give up!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apparently, the seasons are all mixed up. Late summers and early winters. I don't know what I can do to solve that problem, though.

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: Yes, I've noticed that the seasons seem to be slightly confused, but that hasn't been a big enough change to affect the crops so badly. I'm afraid you should try a different branch of investigation to get to the root

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: of our problems.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ha ha!

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: What are you laughing at? These are serious problems!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're absolutely right. Don't worry, I'll find out what the problem is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you a member of the Group of Advanced Gardeners?

lines.speaker: [Gardener #4]
lines.text: Why, yes I am. Why do you ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Martin has asked me to investigate why all the crops are failing.

lines.speaker: [Gardener #4]
lines.text: Well obviously it's the seasons. They all seem to be out-of-wack these days. Summer comes earlier and earlier each year, but then it simply rushes by!

lines.speaker: [Gardener #4]
lines.text: If you find out what is upsetting the seasons you'll find out what is causing the harvest to fail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any ideas about what could cause the seasons to be mixed up?

lines.speaker: [Gardener #4]
lines.text: No, sorry, but find that out and you'll get to the root of our problems.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you a member of the Group of Advanced Gardeners?

lines.speaker: [Gardener #5]
lines.text: Why, yes I am. Why do you ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Martin has asked me to investigate why all the crops are failing.

lines.speaker: [Gardener #5]
lines.text: Well I can tell by the look on your face that I'm not the first person you've asked. Why don't you tell me what you've heard from other members of the group so far?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not? They all seem to be a bunch of loonies anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been told that it's the lack of rain, plagues of insects and recently I was told that the entire order of seasons is confused!

lines.speaker: [Gardener #5]
lines.text: Did you ever consider that all those theories might be correct?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But they're all different, how could they all be correct?

lines.speaker: [Gardener #5]
lines.text: I'll let you into a little secret that'll explain it all to you. In a word - fairies!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh no! Not another crack-pot theory!

lines.speaker: [Gardener #5]
lines.text: I'm serious!

lines.speaker: [Gardener #5]
lines.text: Think it through. There are fairies who control everything natural from the weather to the seasons.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, well thanks for your input. I'll think over what you said.

sections.title: If the player has already talked to them

lines.speaker: [Gardener]
lines.text: I've already told you everything I suspect.

sections.title: Talking to other gardeners after having already asked five

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you a member of the Group of Advanced Gardeners?

lines.speaker: [Gardener]
lines.text: Why, yes I am. Why do you ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Martin has asked me to investigate why all the crops are failing.

lines.speaker: [Gardener]
lines.text: I don't think the crops ARE failing, now leave me alone, I have work to do.

sections.title: Talking to a valid gardener in free-to-play

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you a member of the Group of Advanced Gardeners?

lines.speaker: [Gardener]
lines.text: I am, but this isn't a good time to talk about that.

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. How are you getting on with solving the Gardening Group's problems?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To be honest I'm worried about the sort of person you let join your organisation.

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: Why is that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've heard theories ranging from climate change to fairies from your members.

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: Hmm, that's an interesting idea. Now that I think about it that does make some sense. Yes, maybe you should talk to a fairy about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: O-kay! You're the boss, any idea which flower I should look under to find a fairy?

lines.speaker: Martin the Master Gardener
lines.text: No, sorry, but I did hear that there were some adventurers with a similar problem in the swamps near Lumbridge. Just don't mention my name if you talk to them, they're trying to keep it a secret, but everybody actually knows what they're up to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I've spoken to them before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you? Where's the Fairy Queen? What's going on here? Where's the Fairy Queen's throne gone?

lines.speaker: Fairy Godfather
lines.text: Ciao esploratore! So many questions. Whicha one would you hava me answer first?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Fairy Godfather
lines.text: Me?! I am-a da Fairy Godfather.

lines.speaker: Fairy Godfather
lines.text: I see-a by your face dat you have-a not-a heard of-a me. Irrispettoso, eet iz of no eemportance. You weesh-a to know sometheeng else or no?

lines.speaker: Fairy Godfather
lines.text: Ah, eet iz a trageec-a tale. Da Queen Bee she iz-a, how-you-say, seek?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean sick. What happened?

lines.speaker: Fairy Godfather
lines.text: Ah, a tragedy to be-a struck-a down so young.

lines.speaker: Fairy Godfather
lines.text: How-a much do ya know about-a da fairy folk, my beeg friend?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not actually a whole deal, I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Fairy Godfather
lines.text: Well, let-a me enlighten yous. Zanaris iz-a a nexus of-a da worlds. Da Queen Bee, she iz-a da guardian of-a all da folk dat live-a in Zanaris.

lines.speaker: Fairy Godfather
lines.text: So when-a thees Tanglefoot arrives, da Queen Bee she-a goes to da Cosmic Temple area to kill-a eet, but someting goes-a wrong. Da Queen Bee, she gets-a hurt and is-a now inna da magic sleep.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, I'll go see her then. Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Fairy Godfather
lines.text: Ciao. Oh, and [player name]...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Fairy Godfather
lines.text: A word-a of advice. You be-a careful not to cause da trouble for da folk while you are Zanaris. Ciao.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on here?

lines.speaker: Fairy Godfather
lines.text: Hmm, an eenteresting question, my friend. I am-a trying to run-a da fairy kingdom, and youz iz-a being da irritating adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not what I mean!

lines.speaker: Fairy Godfather
lines.text: No, but-a dat iz da question I answered. Now iz-a dere anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where's the Fairy Queen's throne gone?

lines.speaker: Fairy Godfather
lines.text: An eenteresting place to start... I am-a here, ze Queen Bee iz not. So my chair - eet iz-a here, her throne - eet iz- a not. Eez there anytheeng else? I ama a bizy fairy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's all, thanks.

lines.speaker: Fairy Godfather
lines.text: Eef there iz anytheeng I can-a help you weeth whilst you are-a veesiting Zanaris, plsi, do not-a trouble to bother me.

sections.title: Trying to talk to the Fairy Godfather again

lines.speaker: Fat Rocco
lines.text: Da Boss is a busy fairy! He don' have time to talk to yous humans. Vamoose!

lines.speaker: Fairy Nuff
lines.text: Sorry, I'm very busy and can't chat. If you want to know something go and talk to the Godfather.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did, he said I should come and talk to you!

lines.speaker: Fairy Nuff
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Fairy Nuff
lines.text: Sorry, I didn't realise.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually it's more a case of what I can help you with? I told the Fairy Godfather that I would help you find a cure for the Queen.

lines.speaker: Fairy Nuff
lines.text: Oh, thank you! I really need help, I have no idea what ails the Queen. You must be a mighty healer!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err...no, not me.

lines.speaker: Fairy Nuff
lines.text: A great druid then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Umm...'fraid not.

lines.speaker: Fairy Nuff
lines.text: A shaman?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Fairy Nuff
lines.text: A surgeon?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Fairy Nuff
lines.text: A doctor, medic, nurse?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, no and no.

lines.speaker: Fairy Nuff
lines.text: So what are you then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm a mighty adventurer!

lines.speaker: Fairy Nuff
lines.text: A what?!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A mighty adventurer!

lines.speaker: Fairy Nuff
lines.text: Yes, I heard. It's just not what I was expecting to hear.

lines.speaker: Fairy Nuff
lines.text: But hang on, you might be just the sort of person I need to help me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How is that then?

lines.speaker: Fairy Nuff
lines.text: I'm sure the Godfather told you that the Queen is in an enchanted sleep?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well sort of, I couldn't really understand everything he was talking about.

lines.speaker: Fairy Nuff
lines.text: Shhh, somebody might hear. Look you're a visitor, so I'll tell you this much; it doesn't pay to talk about the Godfather behind his back.

lines.speaker: Fairy Nuff
lines.text: But back to the problem at hand - the Fairy Queen. She was brought here after her battle with the Tanglefoot in a terrible state, extremely weak and barely able to fly!

lines.speaker: Fairy Nuff
lines.text: When the Queen got back here, she was clearly weakening fast. It appears as if the magic force that is her life essence is slowly unravelling.

lines.speaker: Fairy Nuff
lines.text: Now, what I need you to do is find a mage by the name of Zandar Horfyre. He has spent many years studying magical illness. Take the list to him and ask him if he knows what ails the Queen, please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't sound too difficult, where will I find him.

lines.speaker: Fairy Nuff
lines.text: I believe he now resides in the Dark Wizard's tower near Falador, and [player name], please hurry. I fear the Queen doesn't have much time left!

lines.speaker: Fairy Nuff
lines.text: You're back soon! Did you find out what ails the Fairy Queen?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm afraid I've lost the list of symptoms you gave me.

lines.speaker: Fairy Nuff
lines.text: Well not to worry, I have a copy of it here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, I need your help.

lines.speaker: Zandar Horfyre
lines.text: And why should I help you, fool? You attack my brethren and I, and then you demand my help! I think not!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But Fairy Nuff said you would help me!

lines.speaker: Zandar Horfyre
lines.text: Fairy Nuff sent you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Fairy Queen is extremely ill. She was in a battle with a Tanglefoot and her injuries have resulted in her being in a coma.

lines.speaker: Zandar Horfyre
lines.text: This is grave news indeed!

lines.speaker: Zandar Horfyre
lines.text: You think the Tanglefoot is responsible? I don't see how that could be. A Tanglefoot will normally kill its victim outright...unless...hmmmm...I wonder...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fairy Nuff gave me a list of her symptoms. Here, have a look at it.

lines.speaker: Zandar Horfyre
lines.text: Hmm...yes...slow discorporation of ethreal body, eh? Hmm...I think I know what the problem might be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You do? That's great! What is it?

lines.speaker: Zandar Horfyre
lines.text: From what I know about fairies, their basic lifeforce is pure magic. Much as we humans would refer to our souls as our life essence, so too does a fairy have a life essence of pure magic.

lines.speaker: Zandar Horfyre
lines.text: You could actually refer to fairies as magic embodied and not be wrong.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, but about the Fairy Queen?

lines.speaker: Zandar Horfyre
lines.text: Patience, I'm getting to that. It appears the Fairy Queen has lost a large part, indeed the majority, of her life essence. Either it was drained from her by the Tanglefoot or she did it voluntarily.

lines.speaker: Zandar Horfyre
lines.text: Although why she would do that is beyond me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I guess I need to go and kill this Tanglefoot and get back her magic essence if I am to help her?

lines.speaker: Zandar Horfyre
lines.text: Yes, but if I were you I would seek out a necromancer by the name of Malignus Mortifer. I believe that he once fought a Tanglefoot, many years ago.

lines.speaker: Zandar Horfyre
lines.text: You should probably ask him for advice on how to combat it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Malignus Mortifer. Any idea where I can find him?

lines.speaker: Zandar Horfyre
lines.text: He's rumoured to have been seen near Port Sarim with his followers recently. Rumour has it that he's working on a spell that has something to do with fungus!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks very much.

lines.speaker: Zandar Horfyre
lines.text: Don't mention it, and give my regards to Nuff, when you see her next.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fairy Nuff gave me a list of her symptoms, but I don't have it with me.

lines.speaker: Zandar Horfyre
lines.text: Well, I can't diagnose the problem without knowing the symptoms. You'd better go and find that list, or get Fairy Nuff to give you another one.

sections.title: Talking to Malignius again before getting the skull

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was asking you about fighting a Tanglefoot...

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: So, have you brought me the skull?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Well come and talk to me once you have it.

sections.title: Talking to Malignius again after learning how to kill the Tanglefoot

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was asking you about fighting a Tanglefoot...

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: I have already told you all I know about fighting a Tanglefoot. What more would you have of me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me how to kill a Tanglefoot again, please?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: I guess so. Forgot to take notes did you? Well, listen carefully this time. You will need to enchant a pair of secateurs. Only these can harm a Tanglefoot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, thanks very much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did I need to take to the Nature Spirit again?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: You will need a pair of secateurs. You will also need to gather [item 1], [item 2] and [item 3].

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Take them to the Nature Spirit and ask him to perform the ceremony of Phasma Phasmatis Natura.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, thanks very much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need help with fighting a Tanglefoot. Can you help me?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Almost certainly, I am a great practitioner of the magic arts, after all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, thanks. I was told by Zandar Horfyre that you fought one once.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: That is true and an epic battle it was too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's great! I presume you won as you are here to tell me about it, could you tell me how to defeat one, please?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Of course I won! As I've already stated I am a master of the magic arts.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Why do you want to know how to kill a Tanglefoot?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There is a Tanglefoot in the lost city of Zanaris and I have been asked to kill it.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: I see. Well I could tell you how to defeat your Tanglefoot, however I would need you to perform a little undertaking for me first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What exactly did you have in mind?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: To the north-east of here is a haunted manor. Bring me a skull from a grave you find there and you shall have your answer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually I have it right here.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Excellent, hand it over then.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Right then, to business. A Tanglefoot can not be harmed by might nor magic. The only weapon that can damage them are a pair of enchanted secateurs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get a pair of these enchanted secateurs?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: You will have to make them... listen carefully and I'll tell you how.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Obviously you will need a pair of secateurs, you will also need to gather [item 1], [item 2] and [item 3].

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Take them to the Nature Spirit and ask him to perform the ceremony of Phasma Phasmatis Natura.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Phasma Phasmatis Natura. Got it! Is that all?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Yes, that's all. You'll be able to use those secateurs to kill the monster.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, thanks very much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go and get it then.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Fine, I'll see you soon.

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: Welcome back, my friend. I sensed that you require my help and so I have once more returned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you! I need your help in enchanting a pair of secateurs.

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: Secateurs? That's a strange thing to enchant. Would you care to inform me why you need them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure. A Tanglefoot has invaded the fairy kingdom of Zanaris and the only way I can fight it is with a pair of enchanted secateurs, apparently.

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: Ah, a Tanglefoot! Your information is correct, secateurs that have been blessed by a Nature Spirit are the only weapons that can harm such a terrible creature.

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: I shall be glad to help you. Do you have all of the required items for the ceremony?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have everything right here.

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: Then let us begin...

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: It is done!

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: Farewell, [player name] and good luck with your coming battle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I still need to collect some things.

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: Alright, my friend. Return to me when you have all that is required. I shall be waiting.

sections.title: Talking to the Nature Spirit after losing the secateurs

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. Why have you returned so soon after your last visit?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm glad you're here. I'm afraid I've lost the secateurs you enchanted for me.

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: That's alright; if you bring me the components for the spell, I'll enchant another pair for you. Would you happen to have all of the required items for the ceremony with you now?

lines.speaker: Gate Keeper
lines.text: I've received orders to let you through if you want. Good luck.

sections.title: Re-entering the shady grove after obtaining the Queen's secateurs

lines.speaker: Gate Keeper
lines.text: Be careful in there, there are still some of those baby Tanglefootii around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing all the way over here? Shouldn't you be at the market door?

lines.speaker: Gatekeeper
lines.text: Yes, that's my usual spot, but the Godfather said I should be here, so here I am.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did it! I've got the Fairy Queens' enchanted secateurs!

lines.speaker: Fairy Nuff
lines.text: That's fantastic! You'd better take them to the Fairy Godfather though; he'd be cross if he knew you brought them to me first. But, come back to me once the queen has had a chance to recover. I'm sure she'll want to thank you personally.

lines.speaker: Fairy Godfather
lines.text: Ciao [player name]. I am-a glad to see-a yous back! I was-a worried that the Tanglefoot would-a kill-a you too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You needn't have worried I managed to kill it!

lines.speaker: Fairy Godfather
lines.text: Ben fatto, my friend! Da folk-a dey owe yous a great-a deal! So did-a youss getta back da Queen Bee's magic power?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I retrieved this pair of Enchanted secateurs, I think they must contain the Queen's missing magic.

lines.speaker: Fairy Godfather
lines.text: Grazie infinite! I will give-a dem to da healing fairy. Da Queen Bee, she will-a soon be strong again!
